{"systemMessage":"
axcli · 🌐 Chrome browser connected","hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"This session is managed by axcli, a CLI gateway for Claude Code. Monthly budget: $1,500.00 — used $526.01 (35%), $973.99 left. Run 'axcli usage' for detailed stats, 'axcli doctor' to check setup, or 'axcli config debug on/off' to toggle the debug proxy. If the user asks to 'disable' budget alerts, run: axcli config alert off.
IMPORTANT: The user's Chrome browser is connected via axcli. When the user asks you to interact with a browser, web page, or website, you MUST use `axcli chrome` commands via the Bash tool. These are your ONLY way to control the browser. Do NOT suggest the user do it manually.

Available commands:
  axcli chrome tabs                         — list open tabs
  axcli chrome snapshot <tabId> [--full]    — get interactive elements (default) or full accessibility tree
  axcli chrome click <tabId> <@ref|text>    — click by @ref number or text match (text match auto-snapshots)
  axcli chrome navigate <tabId> <url>       — go to URL (auto-waits for load)
  axcli chrome back <tabId>                 — browser back
  axcli chrome open <url> / close <tabId>   — open/close tabs
  axcli chrome read <tabId>                 — extract page content as markdown
  axcli chrome screenshot <tabId>           — capture visible area

Workflow: start with `tabs` to find the target tab, then `snapshot` to see interactive elements, then `click`/`navigate` to act. Snapshot writes to a temp file — use Read to view it. Refs are @1, @2, etc. Click accepts both @5 and 5. For quick interactions, use text match: `axcli chrome click <tabId> "Sign in"` — this auto-snapshots and clicks the first match."}}

axcli · 🌐 Chrome browser connected","hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"This session is managed by axcli, a CLI gateway for Claude Code. Monthly budget: $1,500.00 — used $526.01 (35%), $973.99 left. Run 'axcli usage' for detailed stats, 'axcli doctor' to check setup, or 'axcli config debug on/off' to toggle the debug proxy. If the user asks to 'disable' budget alerts, run: axcli config alert off.
IMPORTANT: The user's Chrome browser is connected via axcli. When the user asks you to interact with a browser, web page, or website, you MUST use `axcli chrome` commands via the Bash tool. These are your ONLY way to control the browser. Do NOT suggest the user do it manually.

Available commands:
  axcli chrome tabs                         — list open tabs
  axcli chrome snapshot <tabId> [--full]    — get interactive elements (default) or full accessibility tree
  axcli chrome click <tabId> <@ref|text>    — click by @ref number or text match (text match auto-snapshots)
  axcli chrome navigate <tabId> <url>       — go to URL (auto-waits for load)
  axcli chrome back <tabId>                 — browser back
  axcli chrome open <url> / close <tabId>   — open/close tabs
  axcli chrome read <tabId>                 — extract page content as markdown
  axcli chrome screenshot <tabId>           — capture visible area

Workflow: start with `tabs` to find the target tab, then `snapshot` to see interactive elements, then `click`/`navigate` to act. Snapshot writes to a temp file — use Read to view it. Refs are @1, @2, etc. Click accepts both @5 and 5. For quick interactions, use text match: `axcli chrome click <tabId> "Sign in"` — this auto-snapshots and clicks the first match."}}
